providers.name: Z.AI
providers.description: Coding-focused provider with dedicated coding-plan support and strong GLM model coverage.
providers.setup_hint: Create a Z.AI API key, then prefer a current GLM coding model.
providers.api_key_help: Z.AI Coding Plan users may need to use the dedicated coding API base instead of the general API.
providers.recommended_model_why: Current flagship GLM coding model with strong tool and agent support.

models.name: GLM-4.5
models.description: Z.AI GLM-4.5 - Previous generation with 128K context window

models.name: GLM-4.5 Air
models.description: Z.AI GLM-4.5 Air - Lightweight version with 128K context window

models.name: GLM-4.6
models.description: Z.AI GLM-4.6 - Coding optimized model with 200K context window

models.name: GLM-4.7
models.description: Z.AI GLM-4.7 - Latest GLM-4.x model with 200K context window

models.name: GLM-5
models.description: Z.AI GLM-5 - Flagship model with 200K context, 128K max output, agentic coding

models.name: GLM-5 Turbo
models.description: Z.AI GLM-5-Turbo - Optimized for agent/tool tasks with 200K context, 128K max output

providers.name: MiniMax
providers.description: Coding-oriented provider with large context windows and dedicated coding-plan options.
providers.setup_hint: Start with MiniMax M2.5 if your account exposes it.
providers.api_key_help: MiniMax supports both standard API access and coding-plan workflows.
providers.recommended_model_why: Best current default in this provider family for coding-oriented use.

providers.name: OpenRouter
providers.description: Unified gateway with many model families and a stable OpenAI-compatible API.
providers.setup_hint: Best for users who want one key and the ability to switch between many coding-capable models.
providers.api_key_help: Choose a coding-capable model after connecting your key.
providers.recommended_model_why: Prefer a coding-focused model over generic defaults.

providers.name: DeepInfra
providers.description: Hosted inference for open models through a straightforward API.
providers.setup_hint: Good fit when you want open-model access without self-hosting.
providers.api_key_help: Create a key, then choose a current coding or reasoning model from the provider list.
providers.recommended_model_why: Good default for coding-oriented open-model inference.

providers.name: Chutes
providers.description: Hosted inference with strong open-model coverage and simple setup.
providers.setup_hint: Use Chutes when you want hosted access to open models with a lightweight workflow.
providers.api_key_help: Create a Chutes key, then pick one of the available coding-capable models.
providers.recommended_model_why: Current coding/reasoning default from the checked-in Chutes catalog.

providers.name: OpenAI
providers.setup_hint: Available, but not recommended as the first onboarding suggestion in this app.
providers.api_key_help: Only configure this if you explicitly want it.
